text: Abortion is the intentional ending of a human life. You don’t call that “choice” or “health care” without lying. Pray, vote, and speak up for the unborn—especially in your church and community.
If you can defend abortion on demand, why can’t you defend a child’s right to life in the womb? Human rights don’t change with party slogans.
Our culture is swallowing the lie that protecting the unborn is “mean.” Real compassion defends life—before it can cry or breathe.
Church: stop treating abortion like politics and start treating it like a crisis of souls. Talk to someone today. Get involved tomorrow.
Abortion destroys families by attacking the weakest among us. Love your neighbor by protecting the unborn—no excuses, no euphemisms.
“Women’s rights” doesn’t mean the state can declare a baby an inconvenience. The right to life must come first.
The Bible says to speak for the helpless. That’s the unborn child. Stand up, pray daily, and encourage others to do the same.
Saying “I’m pro-life but…” is how people hide their compromise. Either you defend life or you don’t—there’s no safe middle.
Abortion isn’t a medical necessity; it’s the taking of an innocent life. Choose compassion with truth, not propaganda with spin.
You can’t call it “reproductive freedom” when it depends on silencing a living person. Stand for life—loudly and prayerfully.
Abortion kills the innocent and wounds the soul of a nation. Real care looks like protecting the unborn, not excusing violence as “choice.” Church, speak up—love demands action.
“Pro-choice” is often code for “pro-abortion,” and then suddenly they demand no debate anywhere else. Justice for the unborn should never be silenced.
Abortion is not compassion. It’s a decision to end a human life. Pray, vote, and get involved—protect moms and protect babies.
Speak up for those who can’t speak. When society stops defending the unborn, it won’t stay “neutral” for long.
Life begins at conception—so abortion is the intentional taking of an innocent life. Stop calling it healthcare; it’s violence.
The same hands that claim to defend women’s rights must also defend the child in the womb. True dignity includes both mother and baby.
If you’re truly pro-woman, you’ll stand for women—and their unborn children—by supporting alternatives to abortion with real help.
Church: don’t just post slogans. Encourage pregnancy support, sidewalk advocacy, and pastoral engagement. Abortion ends when love shows up.
Abortion attacks families because it attacks children. The solution is repentance, prayer, and practical protection for the unborn.
They want “rights” without responsibility. But every right is limited by justice—and the unborn are owed protection, not destruction.
Abortion is not “choice,” it’s the violent end of a living child. Don’t let euphemisms fool you—speak up for the unborn and for justice.
Pro-life isn’t a vibe, it’s a duty. Protect the smallest among us—every life is made by God, not licensed by politicians.
If you can defend killing an unborn child, you’re already accepting the logic of cruelty. Stand against abortion—stop calling it healthcare.
Abortion is a moral crisis disguised as medicine. Pray, vote, and advocate for laws that protect moms and babies.
“But it’s healthcare”—no. Healthcare heals; abortion destroys. Tell the truth and defend those who cannot speak.
Church, we can’t stay silent. Love your neighbor by fighting for life—talk to your pastor and get involved in the pro-life mission.
People who claim to care about women should care about preborn women too—their lives are on the line.
When the unborn are treated as disposable, the whole society becomes less human. Choose life. Reject abortion.
Stop normalizing the killing of children. Lift up pregnancy resource centers, support mothers, and oppose abortion relentlessly.
Abortion dehumanizes the weak. Stand for life, defend the helpless, and let compassion be louder than slogans.
Abortion isn’t compassion—it’s violence disguised as “care.” We need truth, mercy, and protection for the unborn. Speak up, vote pro-life, and support mothers in crisis.
Pro-choice talks about “choice,” but when it’s inconvenient they deny choice to the baby in the womb. Life has priority—always.
The argument that abortion is “health care” ignores the reality: it ends a human life. No amount of slogans changes that. Protect the innocent.
Church, we can’t be silent. Abortion kills children and wounds families—so let’s pray, serve, and advocate with urgency for life.
If you’re pro-life, don’t let anyone shame you into staying quiet. Standing for the unborn is not “mean”—it’s justice.
Planned Parenthood claims “options,” but the option they push most is killing. There are real alternatives—support them and help mothers choose life.
Abortion is the most vulnerable losing. Speak up for those who cannot speak—like Scripture commands.
Heartbreaking: another child erased before anyone can say hello. We must fight for life with prayer, truth, and consistent action.
“Safe, legal, rare” is a lie—abortion isn’t rare, and it’s not safe. Demand accountability and real help for mothers.
You can’t call yourself pro-family while defending abortion. Protect the unborn, defend marriage, and strengthen the home.
Abortion isn’t “choice” when it ends a life. Real compassion protects the unborn—every heartbeat matters. Speak up, vote pro-life, and support pregnancy care in your community.
Church, don’t stay silent. Pro-life action begins with prayer and then with involvement—show up for mothers, mentor fathers, and defend the voiceless.
“A right to life” isn’t a slogan—it’s a moral line. If we can’t protect the innocent, we lose the whole meaning of justice.
Planned Parenthood can’t hide behind slogans. Abortion ends a human life—no amount of rebranding changes that truth.
If someone is truly pro-women, they’ll support mothers with real help—not abortion as a solution. Protect life and protect health.
The unborn are not a “clump of cells.” They’re a child in development. Stand up for them—because God hears the cry of the innocent.
No other procedure is this: the intentional killing of a living child. That’s why abortion is uniquely evil and must be opposed.
We need hearts that care and hands that act: pray for conversion, speak the truth with kindness, and back pro-life policies that save lives.
Stop calling evil “health care.” A society that accepts abortion is choosing darkness over justice—repent and turn back.
Men and women alike must speak up for those who can’t. Proverbs 31 calls us to defend the weak—starting with the unborn.
Abortion kills a child. Don’t dress it up as “choice” or “health care.” Speak up, vote like lives matter, and support women with real care—not excuses.
If you’re truly pro-life, you don’t ignore abortion when it’s inconvenient. Pray, preach, and protect the unborn with action in your church and community.
Abortion destroys families and dehumanizes the weakest among us. Love your neighbor by defending the baby’s right to live—no exceptions.
“Pro-choice” for abortion but “no choice” for religious freedom is hypocrisy. Stand for life, truth, and justice for the innocent.
The lie that abortion is “necessary” never changes the reality: it ends a human life. Refuse propaganda. Choose compassion for the unborn.
Church, the battle is spiritual—and the solution is obedience. Get involved, encourage pregnancy support, and make your voice heard for the unborn.
Stop calling it “care” when it’s violence against an innocent child. Words have meaning—let’s name abortion for what it is.
Laws that permit abortion say one life is worth less. We can do better: protect mothers, support families, and defend children who can’t speak.
Abortion is not empowerment; it’s harm with a political slogan. Be brave enough to tell the truth and show up for life.
Abortion proponents want silence. Don’t give it. Pray for conversion, contact your representatives, and support alternatives that save lives.
Abortion isn’t “choice”—it’s the killing of a child. If you truly care about women, protect life and support real healthcare that doesn’t end a baby. We can do better.
Church, we need to act—pray, speak up, and show up for the unborn. Stand in the gap like salt and light, not silence. Lives depend on it.
Pro-life means defending the most vulnerable: the unborn. Don’t let slogans replace conscience. Speak with courage and compassion—truth matters.
“Abortion is healthcare” is a lie that ends with a baby’s death. No matter what language is used, purpose matters: one life is taken.
If someone supports abortion, they should answer this: why is the unborn excluded from the right to live? Justice doesn’t change because a culture shifts.
The left loves “rights” until the victim is inconvenient. A child in the womb deserves protection under the same moral law.
Parents: choose life, choose support. Every pregnancy deserves help—not judgment. Real care empowers women and protects babies.
Abortion attacks the family—because it denies the humanity of the smallest members. Stand firm for the child, and stand up for truth.
God calls us to speak for those who cannot speak. Pray for an end to abortion and support organizations that show mercy with action.
Don’t be fooled by “pro-choice” rhetoric. Killing is not freedom. The unborn are human, and the law should defend them.
Abortion isn’t “choice” when the choice is to end an unborn child. If you’re truly pro-women, speak up for life—because the baby can’t advocate for itself.
If you can’t defend the unborn in the womb, you don’t get to call yourself pro-life *with conditions*. Protect the vulnerable, period.
Proverbs 31:8–9 is clear: speak up for those who can’t speak. That includes the unborn child being crushed in the womb.
Abortion is the violence they disguise with slogans. Real compassion protects life—not profits from death.
Church, we’ve been silent long enough. Get involved. Pray, volunteer, and support women who choose life—start with the pastors and the people.
To be pro-life means every life: not just the one you like, not just the one that’s convenient. The baby in the womb deserves protection.
“Women’s health” arguments can’t erase the reality: another human life is being intentionally ended. No amount of spin changes that.
People say “don’t judge” — but the unborn child is judged to death. We must speak up with Scripture and courage.
There’s nothing “medical” about ending a beating heart. Call it what it is: abortion is killing.
God’s mercy includes the unborn and the mother. Choose life, support life-affirming care, and stand against the culture of death.
Abortion kills the unborn—plain and simple. Real compassion defends life before birth, not after a tragedy. Pray, vote, and speak up for the helpless. God commands it.
“Pro-choice” doesn’t mean choice when it’s the unborn child paying the price. Protect women by protecting life—period.
Abortion is not “health care.” It’s violence against an innocent child. We should be ashamed that society calls it progress.
If you claim to love freedom, you can’t support killing the smallest and most voiceless. Speak for the unborn—now.
Planned Parenthood doesn’t do “care”—it does abortion. Stand with life and tell the truth outside their doors.
Church, stop treating abortion like a political issue—it's a moral crisis. Get involved locally: prayer, education, and support for mothers.
The Bible is clear: speaking up for the helpless is justice. Abortion silences a person who can’t speak—so we must.
Abortion isn’t complicated—killing innocent life is wrong. Don’t let slogans replace conscience.
Pray to end abortion. Show up, speak up, and love mothers with real help—not with excuses.
Abortion attacks families by destroying children. Stand for life, protect marriage, and build a culture where every baby is valued.
Abortion isn’t “choice.” It’s the intentional ending of a human life. If we believe every person matters, we must protect the unborn—no exceptions, no excuses.
Church, we need more than slogans. Get involved—pray, vote, and support pro-life pregnancy centers. Change must begin in us and spread to our communities.
“Pro-life” means standing up for the voiceless. Speak up for the preborn, just like we would for any neighbor in danger.
Don’t be fooled by euphemisms like “health care.” A baby’s life is at stake. Call abortion what it is: killing an innocent.
Abortion hurts women and families—because it ends lives and leaves real grief behind. Choose mercy, support mothers, and defend the unborn.
Men, women, pastors, and parents—everyone has a role in protecting the defenseless. Pray for courage and speak truth where it’s needed most.
Planned Parenthood isn’t “care.” It’s a machine that profits from death. Stand against it and help alternatives for pregnant moms.
If your politics claim to value life, prove it by defending the unborn. Life isn’t selective—it’s a principle.
“First do no harm” shouldn’t be replaced by abortion. No other procedure is defined by ending a potential life.
God calls us to protect the helpless. Don’t grow numb. Pray. Act. And refuse abortion on demand.
